figures.caption: Figure 4. Temporal specificity of the predictive change-like response. (A) ERPs 2 for Irreg4–4 in the active session (N = 42) are shown for all scalp electrodes. For each 3 electrode, three waveform segments aligned to the nominal middle change point (1 s) 4 are overlaid: pre-mid (gray, −200 to 0 ms), early post-mid (orange, 0 to 200 ms), and 5 late post-mid (black, 300 to 500 ms). (B) Scalp distributions of RM for the same three 6 time windows. The color scale indicates RM magnitude. (C) Participant-level mean 7 RM averaged across the predefined temporal-parietal-occipital ROI for each time 8 window. Horizontal comparisons indicate paired t-tests between time windows (pre vs 9 early post: p < 0.001; early post vs late post: p = 0.02; pre vs late post: p = 0.06). 10

Figure 1. Experimental design and behavioral results. (A) Stimuli and trial 8 structure. Each trial consisted of a 2-s click train comprising two consecutive 1-s 9 segments (Train 1 and Train 2), with a fixed nominal change point at 1 s. Eight 10 stimulus types were presented with equal probability (12.5% each): Reg4–4 (no 11 change), Reg4–4.01/4.02/4.03/4.06 (changes in Train 2 click timing), Irreg4–4 (no change), 12 and Irreg4–4.06 and Irreg4–8 (changes in Train 2 mean timing). In the Irreg conditions, 13 click timing was drawn from a Gaussian distribution with fixed mean values. Click 14

Figure 2. Predictive change-like responses in the no-change stimulus with 2 variable click timing. (A) Grand-average ERPs (N = 42) from five example channels 3 (Fz, T7, Cz, T8, and Pz) for the no-change stimuli Reg4–4 (purple) and Irreg4–4 (orange) 4 in the active session; shaded bands indicate ± s.e.m. The vertical pink line marks the 5 nominal change point (1 s). The blue bar indicates the RM window (1000–1200 ms 6 post-onset). (B) Scalp distributions of ΔRM for Reg4–4 and Irreg4–4. The color scale 7 indicates ΔRM magnitude (warm colors, positive ΔRM; cool colors, negative ΔRM). 8 Black dots denote electrodes with ΔRM significantly greater than zero (one-sample 9

Figure 5. Predictive change-like responses track subjective change reports. (A) 2 Grand-average ERPs (N = 42) from five example channels (Fz, T7, Cz, T8, and Pz) 3 for Irreg4–4 in the active session, separated within participant by behavioral report into 4 trials judged as “change” (red) and trials judged as “no change” (gray); shaded bands 5 indicate ± s.e.m. The vertical pink line marks the nominal change point (1 s). The blue 6 bar indicates the RM window (1000–1200 ms post-onset). (B) Scalp distributions of 7 ΔRM for “change”-report and “no change”-report trials. The color scale indicates 8 ΔRM magnitude (warm colors, positive ΔRM; cool colors, negative ΔRM). Black dots 9 denote electrodes with ΔRM significantly greater than zero (one-sample t-test against 10 zero, FDR-corrected p < 0.05).
